questions.question: The new software will greatly improve the ______ of our customer service team.
questions.explanation: 空格前有定冠詞 the、後面接 of 片語，因此需要名詞。efficiency（效率）是名詞，故為正解。efficient 是形容詞、efficiently 是副詞。
questions.tip: 看到「the ______ of」的結構，中間九成要填名詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 新軟體將大幅提升我們客服團隊的效率。

questions.question: By the time the inspector arrives, the factory ______ all the safety checks.
questions.explanation: 「By the time + 現在式子句」搭配主要子句描述未來某時間點前將完成的動作，須用未來完成式 will have completed。
questions.tip: 看到 By the time...，主要子句常搭配未來完成式或過去完成式，依語意判斷時間軸。
questions.sentenceZh: 等到稽查員抵達時，工廠將已完成所有安全檢查。

questions.question: ______ the heavy rain, the outdoor product launch was moved indoors.
questions.explanation: 空格後接名詞片語 the heavy rain，須使用介系詞片語 Due to。Because 是連接詞，後面必須接完整子句。
questions.tip: 先看空格後面是「名詞」還是「子句」：名詞用 due to / because of，子句用 because / although。
questions.sentenceZh: 由於大雨，戶外產品發表會移到了室內。

questions.question: All employees are required to respond ______ to customer complaints.
questions.explanation: 空格修飾動詞 respond，須用副詞 promptly（迅速地）。
questions.tip: 動詞後面若不缺受詞，空格通常填副詞來修飾動詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 所有員工都必須迅速回應客戶投訴。

questions.question: Ms. Chen asked the interns to submit ______ weekly reports by Friday afternoon.
questions.explanation: 空格後接名詞 weekly reports，需要所有格 their（他們的）。
questions.tip: 空格後緊接名詞時，優先考慮所有格代名詞。
questions.sentenceZh: Chen 要求實習生在週五下午前繳交週報。

questions.question: The board of directors recommended ______ the budget proposal before the end of the quarter.
questions.explanation: recommend 後面接動名詞（V-ing），因此選 approving。同類動詞還有 suggest、consider、avoid 等。
questions.tip: 背口訣：suggest / recommend / consider / avoid / finish 後面接 V-ing。
questions.sentenceZh: 董事會建議在本季結束前核准這份預算提案。

questions.question: The quarterly sales report must be submitted ______ October 15.
questions.explanation: by + 時間點表示「在……之前完成」，強調期限；until 強調動作持續到某時間點，與 submit（一次性動作）語意不合。
questions.tip: 一次性動作（submit、finish）搭配 by；持續性動作（stay、wait）搭配 until。
questions.sentenceZh: 季度銷售報告必須在 10 月 15 日前提交。

questions.question:
Dear Mr. Park,

Thank you for your order of 200 desk lamps. ______, the item is temporarily out of stock. We expect a new shipment to arrive within ten business days.
questions.explanation: 前句感謝訂購，後句說明缺貨（負面消息），語氣轉折須用 Unfortunately（很遺憾地）。
questions.tip: Part 6 轉折詞題先判斷前後句的「語氣方向」：正轉負用 unfortunately / however。
questions.sentenceZh: 很遺憾，該商品暫時缺貨。

questions.question: All gym members are reminded that the swimming pool will be closed for maintenance next week. ______ will resume on Monday, June 8.
questions.explanation: 空格是句子的主詞，且動詞為 will resume（將恢復），語意上是「正常開放時間將於 6 月 8 日恢復」，故選 Regular hours。
questions.tip: Part 6 需要看前後文：前句講「暫停」，後句講「恢復」，主詞應是被暫停的事物。
questions.sentenceZh: 正常營業時間將於 6 月 8 日（週一）恢復。

questions.question: Of all the candidates we interviewed, Ms. Rivera has the ______ experience in international marketing.
questions.explanation: Of all the candidates（在所有應徵者之中）表示三者以上的比較，須用最高級 the most。
questions.tip: 看到 of all / in the world / among 等範圍字眼，用最高級。
questions.sentenceZh: Rivera 在國際行銷方面經驗最豐富。

questions.question: The annual shareholders' meeting ______ in the main conference hall next Tuesday.
questions.explanation: 會議是「被舉行」的，主詞 meeting 與動詞 hold 之間是被動關係，且時間是未來，故用 will be held。
questions.tip: 主詞是「事物」且動詞是 hold / schedule / postpone 時，多半用被動語態。
questions.sentenceZh: 年度股東大會將於下週二在主會議廳舉行。

questions.question: Employees ______ have completed the training course will receive a certificate.
questions.explanation: 先行詞 Employees 是人，且空格在關係子句中作主詞（後面直接接動詞 have completed），故用 who。
questions.tip: 先行詞是人 + 空格後接動詞 → who；空格後接名詞 → whose。
questions.sentenceZh: 完成培訓課程的員工將獲得證書。

questions.question: Ms. Lopez handled the customer's complaint in a very ______ manner.
questions.explanation: 空格在冠詞 a（+副詞 very）與名詞 manner 之間，須填形容詞 professional 來修飾名詞。
questions.tip: 「冠詞 + ______ + 名詞」的空格填形容詞。
questions.sentenceZh: Lopez 以非常專業的方式處理了客戶的投訴。

questions.question: The ______ of the new branch has been delayed until next spring.
questions.explanation: the ______ of 的結構需要名詞，opening（開幕）是名詞，故為正解。
questions.tip: 「the ______ of」中間九成填名詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 新分店的開幕已延後到明年春天。

questions.question: Employees should dress ______ for the client presentation.
questions.explanation: 空格修飾動詞 dress（穿著），須用副詞 appropriately（得體地）。
questions.tip: 不及物動詞後面的空格通常填副詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 員工應為客戶簡報穿著合宜的服裝。

questions.question: The seminar provided ______ information about overseas investment.
questions.explanation: 空格修飾名詞 information，須填形容詞 useful。
questions.tip: 名詞前的空格填形容詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 這場研討會提供了關於海外投資的實用資訊。

questions.question: Our technicians respond to service requests with remarkable ______.
questions.explanation: 介系詞 with + 形容詞 remarkable 之後需要名詞，efficiency（效率）是名詞。
questions.tip: 介系詞後面最終一定要接名詞（中間可夾形容詞）。
questions.sentenceZh: 我們的技術人員以驚人的效率回應維修請求。

questions.question: The marketing director made a ______ decision to delay the launch.
questions.explanation: 空格修飾名詞 decision，須填形容詞 strategic（策略性的）。strategy 是名詞、strategically 是副詞。
questions.tip: 分辨 -ic（形容詞）與 -y（名詞)結尾：strategic 修飾名詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 行銷總監做出了延後上市的策略性決定。

questions.question: All visitors must sign in at the ______ desk.
questions.explanation: reception desk（接待櫃檯）是固定的複合名詞。receptionist 是「接待員」，語意不合。
questions.tip: 複合名詞常考：reception desk、application form、sales report。
questions.sentenceZh: 所有訪客都必須在接待櫃檯簽到。

questions.question: The company's profits grew ______ during the third quarter.
questions.explanation: 空格修飾動詞 grew（成長），須用副詞 significantly（顯著地）。
questions.tip: 動詞後不缺受詞時，空格填副詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 公司獲利在第三季顯著成長。

questions.question: Mr. Tanaka ______ for Horizon Bank since 2015.
questions.explanation: since + 過去時間點，表示從過去持續到現在，用現在完成式 has worked。
questions.tip: 看到 since / for + 一段時間，優先考慮完成式。
questions.sentenceZh: Tanaka 自 2015 年起就在 Horizon 銀行工作。

questions.question: By next March, the construction team ______ the new warehouse.
questions.explanation: By + 未來時間點，表示屆時將完成的動作，用未來完成式 will have completed。
questions.tip: 「By + 未來時間」搭配未來完成式 will have + p.p.。
questions.sentenceZh: 到明年三月，施工團隊將已完成新倉庫。

questions.question: While the technician ______ the printer, the power suddenly went out.
questions.explanation: While 引導的子句描述背景中正在進行的動作，搭配過去進行式 was repairing。
questions.tip: while + 過去進行式（背景動作），主句用過去簡單式（突發事件）。
questions.sentenceZh: 技術人員修理印表機時，電源突然中斷了。

questions.question: The store ______ its grand opening sale next Saturday.
questions.explanation: next Saturday 是未來時間，用未來式 will hold。
questions.tip: 先找時間副詞：next / tomorrow → 未來式。
questions.sentenceZh: 這家店將於下週六舉行盛大開幕特賣。

questions.question: Before she joined our firm, she ______ at a consulting company for five years.
questions.explanation: 「加入本公司」之前更早發生的動作，用過去完成式 had worked 表示「過去的過去」。
questions.tip: 兩個過去動作有先後時，較早的用 had + p.p.。
questions.sentenceZh: 加入我們公司之前，她曾在一家顧問公司工作五年。

questions.question: ______ sales increased last quarter, overall profits remained flat.
questions.explanation: 空格後是完整子句（sales increased...），須用連接詞 Although。Despite / Because of / Owing to 都是介系詞，後面只能接名詞。
questions.tip: 後面是子句→ although；後面是名詞 → despite / because of。
questions.sentenceZh: 雖然上一季銷售成長，整體獲利仍持平。

questions.question: The flight was delayed ______ the heavy snowstorm.
questions.explanation: 空格後是名詞片語 the heavy snowstorm，須用介系詞片語 due to。because / since / as 接子句。
questions.tip: due to + 名詞 = because + 子句。
questions.sentenceZh: 班機因暴風雪而延誤。

questions.question: You may pay by credit card ______ in cash.
questions.explanation: 兩種付款方式擇一，用對等連接詞 or。
questions.tip: 選項並列擇一用 or，皆非用 nor。
questions.sentenceZh: 您可以用信用卡或現金付款。

questions.question: ______ you have any questions, please contact the personnel department.
questions.explanation: Should you have... 是 If you should have... 的倒裝，表示「如果您有任何問題」。
questions.tip: 句首的 Should + 主詞 = If 假設句的正式倒裝寫法。
questions.sentenceZh: 如有任何問題，請聯絡人事部門。

questions.question: The report must be both accurate ______ concise.
questions.explanation: both A and B 是固定搭配，表示「既 A 且 B」。
questions.tip: 看到 both 就找 and；either 找 or；neither 找 nor。
questions.sentenceZh: 報告必須既正確又簡潔。

questions.question: The workshop has been rescheduled ______ May 12.
questions.explanation: reschedule A for + 新日期 是固定用法，表示「改期到某日」。
questions.tip: be scheduled / rescheduled for + 日期，是多益高頻搭配。
questions.sentenceZh: 工作坊已改期至 5 月 12 日。

questions.question: Please respond to this invitation ______ Friday.
questions.explanation: respond（回覆）是一次性動作，搭配 by（在…之前）表示期限。until 用於持續性動作。
questions.tip: 一次性動作 + by；持續性動作 + until。
questions.sentenceZh: 請在週五前回覆這份邀請。

questions.question: The office will be closed ______ the national holiday.
questions.explanation: 空格後是名詞片語 the national holiday，用介系詞 during。while / when 後面接子句。
questions.tip: during + 名詞；while + 子句。
questions.sentenceZh: 辦公室在國定假日期間將關閉。

questions.question: Ms. Patel is responsible ______ training the new interns.
questions.explanation: be responsible for（負責…）是固定搭配。
questions.tip: 背固定搭配：responsible for、famous for、apologize for。
questions.sentenceZh: Patel 負責訓練新進實習生。

questions.question: The two managers finally agreed ______ a new pricing plan.
questions.explanation: agree on + 事項（就…達成一致）是固定搭配；agree with + 人。
questions.tip: agree on 事情、agree with 人、agree to 提議。
questions.sentenceZh: 兩位經理終於就新的定價方案達成共識。

questions.question: Employees may bring ______ own laptops to the training.
questions.explanation: own 前面必須用所有格，their own（他們自己的）。
questions.tip: 「______ + own + 名詞」固定用所有格。
questions.sentenceZh: 員工可以自帶筆電參加培訓。

questions.question: The design proposal was praised by everyone who reviewed ______.
questions.explanation: reviewed 的受詞指的是單數的 the design proposal，用受格 it。
questions.tip: 先找代名詞指涉的對象，確認單複數再選。
questions.sentenceZh: 這份設計提案受到所有審閱者的讚賞。

questions.question: Ms. Chen prepared the annual report ______ because her assistant was on leave.
questions.explanation: 強調「親自做」用反身代名詞 herself。
questions.tip: 句子已完整、空格只是強調「親自」→ 反身代名詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 因為助理請假，Chen 親自準備了年度報告。

questions.question: ______ of the two candidates has strong leadership experience.
questions.explanation: 動詞是單數 has，Both / All 接複數動詞；Neither of the two（兩者皆非）搭配單數動詞。
questions.tip: 看動詞單複數反推主詞：has → Neither / Each。
questions.sentenceZh: 兩位候選人都沒有很強的領導經驗。

questions.question: Those ______ wish to attend the banquet should register by Friday.
questions.explanation: Those who...（想要…的人）是固定句型，who 在子句中作主詞。
questions.tip: Those who + 動詞 =「…的人們」，多益常客。
questions.sentenceZh: 想參加晚宴的人應在週五前報名。

questions.question: The committee agreed ______ the proposal at next week's session.
questions.explanation: agree to + 原形動詞（同意做…）是固定用法。
questions.tip: agree / decide / plan / hope 後面接 to V。
questions.sentenceZh: 委員會同意在下週會議上討論這項提案。

questions.question: We look forward to ______ from you soon.
questions.explanation: look forward to 的 to 是介系詞，後面接動名詞 hearing。
questions.tip: look forward to / be used to / object to 後面接 V-ing。
questions.sentenceZh: 我們期待盡快收到您的回音。

questions.question: The manager suggested ______ the deadline by one week.
questions.explanation: suggest 後面接動名詞 extending。
questions.tip: suggest / recommend / consider / avoid + V-ing。
questions.sentenceZh: 經理建議把截止日期延長一週。

questions.question: All staff are required ______ the safety training annually.
questions.explanation: be required to + 原形動詞（被要求做…）是固定句型。
questions.tip: be required / expected / encouraged to V。
questions.sentenceZh: 所有員工每年都必須完成安全訓練。

questions.question: Would you mind ______ the window? It's a bit cold in here.
questions.explanation: mind 後面接動名詞 closing。
questions.tip: mind / enjoy / finish + V-ing。
questions.sentenceZh: 您介意關上窗戶嗎？

questions.question: The invoices ______ to clients at the beginning of each month.
questions.explanation: 主詞 invoices 是「被寄出」的，且是每月例行性動作，用現在簡單式被動 are sent。
questions.tip: 主詞是物且不會自己做動作 → 被動語態。
questions.sentenceZh: 發票在每月月初寄給客戶。

questions.question: The new policy ______ by the board at yesterday's meeting.
questions.explanation: 政策是「被核准」的，且時間是昨天，用過去式被動 was approved。空格後的 by 是被動語態的線索。
questions.tip: 看到「by + 執行者」幾乎可確定是被動語態。
questions.sentenceZh: 新政策在昨天的會議上獲得董事會核准。

questions.question: All luggage must ______ before boarding the aircraft.
questions.explanation: 行李是「被檢查」的，助動詞 must 後接原形，被動為 must be inspected。
questions.tip: 助動詞 + be + p.p. 是被動的固定形式。
questions.sentenceZh: 所有行李在登機前都必須接受檢查。

questions.question: The candidate ______ resume impressed us most will be interviewed first.
questions.explanation: 空格後接名詞 resume，表示「他的履歷」，用所有格關代 whose。
questions.tip: 關代空格後緊接名詞 → whose。
questions.sentenceZh: 履歷最令我們印象深刻的候選人將優先面試。

questions.question: The hotel ______ we stayed at last year has been renovated.
questions.explanation: 子句中已有介系詞 at（stayed at ___），空格作 at 的受詞，用 which。若用 where 則不能再加 at。
questions.tip: 子句尾已有介系詞 → 用 which/that；沒有介系詞 → 才用 where。
questions.sentenceZh: 我們去年住過的那家飯店已經翻新了。

questions.question: Applicants ______ meet all the requirements will be contacted next week.
questions.explanation: 先行詞 Applicants 是人，空格後直接接動詞 meet，關代作主詞用 who。
questions.tip: 先行詞是人 + 空格後接動詞 → who。
questions.sentenceZh: 符合所有條件的申請者將於下週收到通知。

questions.question: The new model is far more energy-efficient ______ the previous one.
questions.explanation: more... 比較級固定搭配 than。
questions.tip: 看到 more / -er 就找 than。
questions.sentenceZh: 新機型比前一代節能得多。

questions.question: This is ______ conference room available in the building.
questions.explanation: in the building 限定了比較範圍（整棟樓之中），用最高級 the largest。
questions.tip: in / of + 範圍 → 最高級，且最高級前要加 the。
questions.sentenceZh: 這是本大樓可用的最大會議室。

questions.question: The sooner you register, ______ your chances of getting a seat.
questions.explanation: 「The + 比較級..., the + 比較級...」（越…就越…）是固定句型。
questions.tip: The sooner..., the better... 前後都要有 the + 比較級。
questions.sentenceZh: 越早報名，搶到座位的機會就越大。

questions.question: The results of the survey were quite ______.
questions.explanation: 主詞是事物（results），事物「令人驚訝」用現在分詞 surprising；人感到驚訝才用 surprised。
questions.tip: 事物用 -ing（令人…），人用 -ed（感到…）。
questions.sentenceZh: 調查結果相當令人驚訝。

questions.question: ______ by the heavy workload, several employees requested assistance.
questions.explanation: 員工是「被工作量壓垮」的，分詞構句用過去分詞 Overwhelmed。
questions.tip: 分詞構句：主詞被動作影響 → p.p. 開頭；主動做動作 → V-ing 開頭。
questions.sentenceZh: 由於工作量過重，數名員工請求支援。

questions.question: Anyone ______ in volunteering should contact Ms. Rivera.
questions.explanation: 人「感到有興趣」用過去分詞 interested，且 be interested in 是固定搭配。
questions.tip: interested in（人有興趣）vs interesting（事物有趣）。
questions.sentenceZh: 有興趣擔任志工的人請聯絡 Rivera 女士。

questions.question: ______ information about the merger will be released next week.
questions.explanation: information 是不可數名詞，Many / Several / A few 只能接可數複數；Further（更多的）可接不可數名詞。
questions.tip: information、advice、equipment 都不可數，不能用 many / few。
questions.sentenceZh: 關於合併案的進一步資訊將於下週公布。

questions.question: There are only a ______ tickets left for the awards dinner.
questions.explanation: tickets 是可數複數，用 a few（少數幾張）。a little 接不可數名詞。
questions.tip: a few + 可數複數；a little + 不可數。
questions.sentenceZh: 頒獎晚宴的門票只剩少數幾張。

questions.question: ______ of the equipment in the lab needs to be replaced.
questions.explanation: equipment 是不可數名詞，且動詞 needs 是單數，用 Much。
questions.tip: equipment / furniture / luggage 永遠不可數。
questions.sentenceZh: 實驗室裡大部分的設備都需要汰換。

questions.question: If the shipment ______ late again, we will switch suppliers.
questions.explanation: 第一類條件句（未來很可能發生）：If + 現在式，主句 will + 原形。主詞 shipment 是單數，用 arrives。
questions.tip: If 子句用現在式代替未來式，不能放 will。
questions.sentenceZh: 如果貨件再次遲到，我們就會更換供應商。

questions.question: If Ms. Wong had checked the schedule, she ______ the meeting.
questions.explanation: 與過去事實相反的假設：If + had p.p., 主句 would have p.p.。
questions.tip: 看到 If + had p.p.，主句必配 would/could have p.p.。
questions.sentenceZh: 如果 Wong 女士當時查過行程表，就不會錯過會議了。

questions.question: Thank you for your interest in our premium membership. ______, we are offering a 20 percent discount to all new members this month.
questions.explanation: 後句是促銷優惠內容，前後沒有因果或轉折關係，用 For a limited time（限時）最符合廣告語氣。
questions.tip: Part 6 先判斷前後句關係：沒有轉折/因果時，找語意上最自然的副詞片語。
questions.sentenceZh: 在限定期間內，本月我們提供所有新會員八折優惠。

questions.question: The elevator in Building C will be under repair on Monday. ______, please use the stairs or the elevator in Building B.
questions.explanation: 前句說明電梯維修，後句是「這段期間」的替代方案，用 In the meantime（在此期間）。
questions.tip: 前句講「暫時狀況」、後句給「過渡做法」→ in the meantime。
questions.sentenceZh: 在此期間，請使用樓梯或 B 棟的電梯。

questions.question: We have received your payment of $250. ______, your order has been shipped and should arrive within five business days.
questions.explanation: 收到付款 → 因此出貨，前後是因果順承關係，用 Accordingly（因此；據此）。
questions.tip: 正向因果用 accordingly / therefore；轉折才用 however。
questions.sentenceZh: 因此，您的訂單已出貨，應會在五個工作天內送達。

questions.question: All employees are invited to the year-end party. ______ who plan to attend should reply by December 1.
questions.explanation: 空格後接 who 引導的關係子句，Those who（凡是…的人）是固定搭配；Anyone / Everyone 接 who 時動詞用單數，但後句 plan 是複數。
questions.tip: 「______ who + 複數動詞」→ Those。
questions.sentenceZh: 計畫出席的人請在 12 月 1 日前回覆。

questions.question: Please read the safety instructions ______ before using the equipment.
questions.explanation: 空格修飾動詞 read，用副詞 carefully。
questions.tip: 動詞 + 受詞之後的空格常填副詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 使用設備前請仔細閱讀安全說明。

questions.question: The hotel is known for its exceptional ______ to detail.
questions.explanation: 所有格 its + 形容詞 exceptional 之後需要名詞，attention to detail（對細節的注重）是固定搭配。
questions.tip: 「所有格 + 形容詞 + ______」填名詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 這家飯店以對細節的卓越關注而聞名。

questions.question: The two firms formed a ______ partnership last year.
questions.explanation: 空格修飾名詞 partnership，用形容詞 profitable（有利可圖的）。
questions.tip: 冠詞與名詞之間填形容詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 這兩家公司去年建立了獲利豐厚的合作關係。

questions.question: Ticket holders must show valid ______ at the entrance.
questions.explanation: 形容詞 valid 之後、作 show 的受詞，需要名詞 identification（身分證件）。
questions.tip: 動詞的受詞位置填名詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 持票者必須在入口出示有效證件。

questions.question: The CEO spoke ______ about the company's future plans.
questions.explanation: 空格修飾動詞 spoke，用副詞 confidently（有自信地）。
questions.tip: spoke / responded / performed 這類動詞後常接副詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 執行長自信地談到公司的未來計畫。

questions.question: There has been a steady ______ in online orders since April.
questions.explanation: 冠詞 a + 形容詞 steady 之後需要名詞 increase（成長）。
questions.tip: an increase / decrease in + 名詞，是多益高頻句型。
questions.sentenceZh: 自四月以來，線上訂單持續穩定成長。

questions.question: The workshop will help managers communicate more ______ with their teams.
questions.explanation: 空格修飾動詞 communicate，用副詞 effectively，more effectively 為比較級。
questions.tip: more + 副詞也是常見結構，別看到 more 就以為要選形容詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 這場工作坊將幫助主管與團隊更有效地溝通。

questions.question: Please complete the attached form and return it at your earliest ______.
questions.explanation: at your earliest convenience（在您方便時盡早）是商業書信固定用語，形容詞 earliest 後接名詞。
questions.tip: 背整句：at your earliest convenience ＝ 請盡快。
questions.sentenceZh: 請填妥附件表格，並儘早回傳。

questions.question: The maintenance crew ______ the air conditioning system right now.
questions.explanation: right now（此刻）表示正在進行，用現在進行式 is inspecting。
questions.tip: right now / at the moment → 現在進行式。
questions.sentenceZh: 維修人員現在正在檢查空調系統。

questions.question: Our company ______ three new branches since January.
questions.explanation: since January 表示從一月至今的累積成果，用現在完成式 has opened。
questions.tip: since + 時間點 → 完成式。
questions.sentenceZh: 自一月以來，我們公司已開設三家新分店。

questions.question: When the fire alarm rang, everyone ______ the building immediately.
questions.explanation: when 子句是過去式 rang，主句同樣描述過去動作，用過去式 left。
questions.tip: 前後子句時態要一致：過去配過去。
questions.sentenceZh: 火警警報響起時，所有人立刻離開了大樓。

questions.question: The train ______ at platform six in ten minutes.
questions.explanation: 火車時刻表等既定行程可用現在簡單式表未來，arrives 為正解。
questions.tip: 時刻表、營業時間等固定行程 → 現在簡單式表未來。
questions.sentenceZh: 列車十分鐘後抵達第六月台。

questions.question: ______ the rain stops soon, the outdoor event will be canceled.
questions.explanation: 語意是「除非雨很快停，否則活動將取消」，用 Unless（除非）。用 If 語意矛盾。
questions.tip: Unless = If not，選前先把語意帶進去讀一次。
questions.sentenceZh: 除非雨很快停，否則戶外活動將取消。

questions.question: The package arrived on time ______ the shipping delays.
questions.explanation: 空格後是名詞片語 the shipping delays，且語意是「儘管延誤仍準時」，用介系詞 despite。
questions.tip: despite + 名詞（讓步）；although + 子句。
questions.sentenceZh: 儘管運送延誤，包裹仍準時送達。

questions.question: Neither the manager ______ the assistant knew about the schedule change.
questions.explanation: neither A nor B（A 和 B 都不）是固定搭配。
questions.tip: neither 必配 nor，either 必配 or。
questions.sentenceZh: 經理和助理都不知道行程變更。

questions.question: Keep the receipt ______ you need to return the item.
questions.explanation: in case（以防萬一）符合「留著收據以防需要退貨」的語意。
questions.tip: in case = 以防；so that = 以便；now that = 既然。
questions.sentenceZh: 請保留收據，以備需要退貨時使用。

questions.question: ______ he had finished the report, Mr. Silva left the office.
questions.explanation: 空格後是完整子句，語意為「一旦完成報告就離開」，用連接詞 Once。During / Despite 是介系詞。
questions.tip: Once + 子句 = 一…就…。
questions.sentenceZh: 完成報告後，Silva 先生就離開了辦公室。

questions.question: The seminar will begin ______ 2:00 p.m. sharp.
questions.explanation: 確切時刻用 at：at 2:00 p.m.。
questions.tip: at + 時刻、on + 日期、in + 月份/年份。
questions.sentenceZh: 研討會將於下午兩點開始。

questions.question: Our sales have doubled ______ the past two years.
questions.explanation: over the past two years（在過去兩年間）是固定搭配，表示一段期間內的變化。
questions.tip: over / during / in the past + 一段時間，搭配完成式。
questions.sentenceZh: 過去兩年我們的銷售額翻了一倍。

questions.question: The new intern is in charge ______ sorting the mail.
questions.explanation: in charge of（負責…）是固定搭配。
questions.tip: in charge of = responsible for。
questions.sentenceZh: 新來的實習生負責整理信件。

questions.question: Passengers should arrive at the airport two hours ______ departure.
questions.explanation: prior to（在…之前）= before，是多益常見的正式用語。
questions.tip: prior to = before，常出現在公告與指示。
questions.sentenceZh: 乘客應在起飛前兩小時抵達機場。

questions.question: The bakery is located ______ the bank and the post office.
questions.explanation: 兩者之間用 between（between A and B）；三者以上才用 among。
questions.tip: between 兩者、among 三者以上。
questions.sentenceZh: 麵包店位於銀行和郵局之間。

questions.question: The IT department finished ______ the new software yesterday.
questions.explanation: finish 後面接動名詞 installing。
questions.tip: finish / enjoy / keep + V-ing。
questions.sentenceZh: 資訊部門昨天完成了新軟體的安裝。

questions.question: Ms. Novak decided ______ the job offer in Singapore.
questions.explanation: decide to + 原形動詞是固定用法。
questions.tip: decide / plan / agree / hope + to V。
questions.sentenceZh: Novak 決定接受新加坡的工作邀約。

questions.question: The security guard let the visitors ______ the building after checking their IDs.
questions.explanation: 使役動詞 let + 受詞 + 原形動詞。
questions.tip: let / make / have + 受詞 + 原形 V。
questions.sentenceZh: 警衛查驗證件後讓訪客進入大樓。

questions.question: Please avoid ______ personal calls during work hours.
questions.explanation: avoid 後面接動名詞 making。
questions.tip: avoid / consider / suggest + V-ing。
questions.sentenceZh: 請避免在上班時間撥打私人電話。

questions.question: The company encourages employees ______ public transportation.
questions.explanation: encourage + 受詞 + to V（鼓勵某人做…）是固定句型。
questions.tip: encourage / allow / advise / remind + 人 + to V。
questions.sentenceZh: 公司鼓勵員工搭乘大眾運輸工具。

questions.question: The awards ceremony ______ live on the company website tonight.
questions.explanation: 典禮是「被轉播」的，時間為 tonight（未來），用 will be broadcast。
questions.tip: 主詞是活動/事物 + 轉播/舉行類動詞 → 被動。
questions.sentenceZh: 頒獎典禮今晚將在公司網站上現場直播。

questions.question: Applications ______ until the position is filled.
questions.explanation: 申請書是「被受理」的，且是持續到未來的狀態，用 will be accepted。
questions.tip: 招募公告常見句：Applications will be accepted until...。
questions.sentenceZh: 職缺補滿之前將持續受理申請。

questions.question: The conference room should ______ for the client visit tomorrow morning.
questions.explanation: 會議室是「被準備」的，should 後接 be + p.p.。
questions.tip: 房間/文件/設備當主詞時，十之八九是被動。
questions.sentenceZh: 會議室應在明天早上客戶來訪前準備妥當。

questions.question: The consultant ______ we hired last month has already improved our workflow.
questions.explanation: 先行詞 consultant 是人，且在子句中作 hired 的受詞（we hired him），用受格 whom。
questions.tip: 關代後面接「主詞 + 動詞」→ 關代作受詞，人用 whom。
questions.sentenceZh: 我們上個月聘請的顧問已經改善了我們的工作流程。

questions.question: The factory, ______ was built in 1998, now employs over 500 workers.
questions.explanation: 逗號後的非限定關係子句不能用 that，先行詞是物，用 which。
questions.tip: 看到「, ______」的非限定用法，直接刪掉 that。
questions.sentenceZh: 這座工廠建於 1998 年，如今僱用超過五百名員工。

questions.question: This is the restaurant ______ the farewell dinner will be held.
questions.explanation: 子句 the dinner will be held 結構完整（不缺主受詞），需要表示地點的關係副詞 where。
questions.tip: 子句完整不缺格 → 用 where / when；缺主詞或受詞 → 用 which / that。
questions.sentenceZh: 這就是歡送晚宴將舉行的餐廳。

questions.question: Of the three printers, this model operates the ______ quietly.
questions.explanation: 三者比較用最高級，副詞的最高級為 the most quietly。
questions.tip: Of the three... → 最高級。
questions.sentenceZh: 在這三台印表機中，這一款運轉最安靜。

questions.question: The revised proposal is much ______ than the original version.
questions.explanation: 有 than，用比較級 clearer；much 用來加強比較級。
questions.tip: much / far / even + 比較級（加強語氣）。
questions.sentenceZh: 修訂後的提案比原始版本清楚得多。

questions.question: No other branch performed ______ well as the downtown location this year.
questions.explanation: as... as 原級比較：No other... as well as ~ 表示「沒有其他分店表現得和市中心店一樣好」。
questions.tip: No other + as...as = 實質上的最高級。
questions.sentenceZh: 今年沒有任何分店的表現比得上市中心分店。

questions.question: The company offers ______ prices on all bulk orders.
questions.explanation: 價格是「被降低」的，用過去分詞 reduced 作形容詞修飾 prices。
questions.tip: reduced prices、attached file、limited time — 被動意味用 p.p.。
questions.sentenceZh: 本公司對所有大量訂購提供優惠價格。

questions.question: ______ the annual report, the accountant found a serious error.
questions.explanation: 會計師主動「審閱」報告，分詞構句用現在分詞 Reviewing。
questions.tip: 分詞構句：主詞主動做 → V-ing；被動承受 → p.p.。
questions.sentenceZh: 審閱年度報告時，會計師發現了一個嚴重錯誤。

questions.question: The training session was so ______ that many attendees fell asleep.
questions.explanation: 課程「令人無聊」用現在分詞 boring；人「感到無聊」才用 bored。
questions.tip: 主詞是事物 → -ing；主詞是人 → -ed。
questions.sentenceZh: 培訓課程無聊到許多與會者都睡著了。

questions.question: ______ employee must attend at least one training session per year.
questions.explanation: 空格後是單數名詞 employee 且動詞為 must attend，用 Each（每一位）。All / Both / Many 接複數。
questions.tip: Each / Every + 單數名詞；All / Many + 複數。
questions.sentenceZh: 每位員工每年必須至少參加一場培訓。

questions.question: We have received ______ complaints about the new phone system.
questions.explanation: complaints 是可數複數，用 several（好幾件）。much / a little 接不可數。
questions.tip: 先判斷名詞可不可數，再挑數量詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 我們收到了數起關於新電話系統的投訴。

questions.question: There is ______ time left before the submission deadline.
questions.explanation: time 是不可數名詞，且語意是「所剩不多」，用 little。
questions.tip: little（幾乎沒有）vs a little（還有一點）語氣不同。
questions.sentenceZh: 距離繳交期限只剩下很少的時間。

questions.question: If I ______ you, I would double-check the figures before submitting.
questions.explanation: 與現在事實相反的假設，be 動詞一律用 were：If I were you。
questions.tip: If I were you 是固定用法，不用 was。
questions.sentenceZh: 如果我是你，我會在提交前再核對一次數字。

questions.question: ______ the CEO arrive early, please show her to the boardroom.
questions.explanation: 空格後動詞是原形 arrive，這是 If the CEO should arrive 的倒裝：Should the CEO arrive。若選 If，動詞須為 arrives。
questions.tip: 主詞後接原形動詞 → 想到 Should 倒裝。
questions.sentenceZh: 若執行長提早抵達，請帶她到董事會議室。

questions.question: The meeting has been called ______ due to the typhoon warning.
questions.explanation: call off（取消）是固定片語動詞。
questions.tip: call off = cancel，多益高頻片語。
questions.sentenceZh: 會議因颱風警報而取消。

questions.question: Please fill ______ this form and hand it to the receptionist.
questions.explanation: fill in（填寫）是固定片語動詞（美式亦常用 fill out）。
questions.tip: fill in / fill out 都是「填寫」。
questions.sentenceZh: 請填寫這份表格並交給接待人員。

questions.question: We apologize ______ any inconvenience this may cause.
questions.explanation: apologize for + 事情（為…道歉）是固定搭配；apologize to + 人。
questions.tip: 公告常見句：We apologize for any inconvenience.。
questions.sentenceZh: 對於由此造成的任何不便，我們深感抱歉。

questions.question: The new regulations will take ______ on the first of July.
questions.explanation: take effect（生效）是固定搭配；take place 是「舉行」。
questions.tip: 法規生效 take effect；活動舉行 take place。
questions.sentenceZh: 新規定將於 7 月 1 日生效。

questions.question: Ms. Ito will take ______ of the project while the director is away.
questions.explanation: take charge of（接管；負責）是固定搭配。
questions.tip: take charge of = 接手負責；take part in = 參加。
questions.sentenceZh: 總監不在期間，Ito 將負責這項專案。

questions.question:
Dear valued customer,

Our online store will be temporarily unavailable this Sunday. ______, we will be upgrading our payment system to serve you better.
questions.explanation: 前句說明週日暫停服務，後句解釋「這段期間」要做的升級作業，用 During this time。
questions.tip: 找前後句的時間關係：暫停期間做某事 → during this time。
questions.sentenceZh: 在此期間，我們將升級付款系統，以提供您更好的服務。

questions.question: We appreciate your patience while we process your request. ______, you can track its status on our website.
questions.explanation: 處理期間可以先做的事，用 In the meantime（在此期間）銜接最自然。
questions.tip: 「等待期間可以…」→ in the meantime。
questions.sentenceZh: 在此期間，您可以在我們網站上追蹤它的狀態。

questions.question: The fitness center's opening hours will be extended starting next month. ______ change reflects feedback from our members.
questions.explanation: 指前一句提到的單一變更（延長營業時間），用單數指示詞 This change。
questions.tip: Part 6 指示詞題：先確認指的是前文哪件事、單數還是複數。
questions.sentenceZh: 這項變更反映了會員們的意見回饋。

questions.question: All interns will receive a certificate upon completing the program. ______, outstanding interns may be offered full-time positions.
questions.explanation: 後句在前句的好處上追加另一項好處，屬於遞進關係，用 Furthermore（此外）。
questions.tip: 前後同方向加碼 → furthermore / in addition；方向相反 → however。
questions.sentenceZh: 此外，表現優異的實習生可能獲得正職職位。

questions.question: Thank you for submitting your manuscript. Our editors will review it within four weeks. ______ it is accepted, you will receive a publishing contract by email.
questions.explanation: 後句是條件關係：「如果被採用，就會收到合約」，用 If。
questions.tip: 條件 + 結果的句型，優先試 If 帶入讀看看。
questions.sentenceZh: 若獲採用，您將透過電子郵件收到出版合約。

questions.question: The two companies signed a(n) ______ to share research facilities.
questions.explanation: sign an agreement（簽署協議）是固定搭配；appliance 是家電、attendance 是出席。
questions.tip: 字彙題先看動詞搭配：sign 後面接 agreement / contract。
questions.sentenceZh: 兩家公司簽署了共享研究設施的協議。

questions.question: Employees must wear protective ______ when entering the laboratory.
questions.explanation: protective equipment（防護裝備）是固定搭配。
questions.tip: protective 後面常接 equipment / gear / clothing。
questions.sentenceZh: 進入實驗室時，員工必須穿戴防護裝備。

questions.question: The hotel offers a free shuttle ______ to the convention center.
questions.explanation: shuttle service（接駁服務）是固定搭配。
questions.tip: 選項長得像時，找「和前面名詞組成慣用語」的那個。
questions.sentenceZh: 飯店提供往返會議中心的免費接駁車服務。

questions.question: Due to high ______, the seminar has been moved to a larger room.
questions.explanation: high demand（需求踴躍）符合「換到更大教室」的因果關係。
questions.tip: 字彙題把每個選項帶回句子讀，只有一個語意通順。
questions.sentenceZh: 由於需求踴躍，研討會已改到更大的教室。

questions.question: Please ______ the enclosed form and return it by May 3.
questions.explanation: complete the form（填妥表格）是商業書信高頻用語；compete 是競爭。
questions.tip: form 的搭配動詞：complete / fill in / submit。
questions.sentenceZh: 請填妥隨函附上的表格，並於 5 月 3 日前寄回。

questions.question: The committee will ______ the proposals and select a winner next week.
questions.explanation: 「審核提案並選出優勝者」用 evaluate（評估）；evacuate 是疏散、elevate 是提升。
questions.tip: 小心形近字：evaluate（評估）vs evacuate（疏散）。
questions.sentenceZh: 委員會將評估提案並在下週選出優勝者。

questions.question: Ms. Park was praised for her ______ to the project.
questions.explanation: dedication to（對…的投入）是固定搭配；deduction 是扣除。
questions.tip: dedication / commitment + to，介系詞是線索。
questions.sentenceZh: Park 因對專案的投入而受到表揚。

questions.question: The airline offers a generous baggage ______ for business class.
questions.explanation: baggage allowance（行李額度）是航空業固定用語。
questions.tip: allowance = 額度/津貼，多益常考 baggage / travel allowance。
questions.sentenceZh: 這家航空公司為商務艙提供慷慨的行李額度。

questions.question: Sales ______ for the northern region will be announced on Friday.
questions.explanation: sales figures（銷售數據）是固定搭配。
questions.tip: figures 當「數據」解是多益高頻義。
questions.sentenceZh: 北區的銷售數字將於週五公布。

questions.question: The restaurant received an award for its ______ service.
questions.explanation: outstanding service（傑出的服務）符合「獲獎」的語意；outdated 是過時的。
questions.tip: out- 開頭形近字要靠語意分辨：outstanding 傑出、outdated 過時。
questions.sentenceZh: 這家餐廳因傑出的服務而獲獎。

questions.question: ______ had the meeting started when the fire alarm went off.
questions.explanation: Hardly had + S + p.p. ... when...（才剛…就…）是固定倒裝句型。
questions.tip: 句首否定副詞（Hardly / Never / Seldom）→ 主詞動詞倒裝。
questions.sentenceZh: 會議才剛開始，火警警報就響了。

questions.question: Not only ______ the deadline, but she also improved the design.
questions.explanation: Not only 放句首時須倒裝：Not only did she meet...。
questions.tip: Not only 在句首 → 後面接助動詞 + 主詞 + 原形動詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 她不僅趕上了期限，還改良了設計。

questions.question: The manager has not decided ______ the launch will be delayed.
questions.explanation: 空格後是完整子句，表示「是否」用 whether；what / which / who 需要在子句中擔任成分。
questions.tip: 子句完整不缺格 → whether / that；缺主詞或受詞 → what / who。
questions.sentenceZh: 經理尚未決定是否延後上市。

questions.question: ______ surprised the investors was the sudden drop in profits.
questions.explanation: What surprised the investors（令投資人驚訝的事）作主詞，What 在子句中兼任主詞。
questions.tip: 「______ + 動詞 + 的事」當主詞 → What。
questions.sentenceZh: 讓投資人吃驚的是獲利突然下滑。

questions.question: ______ wins the contract will need to start work immediately.
questions.explanation: Whoever（無論誰）= Anyone who，引導名詞子句作整句主詞。
questions.tip: 沒有先行詞時用 whoever / whichever / whatever。
questions.sentenceZh: 無論誰贏得這份合約，都必須立即開始工作。

questions.question: ______ in 1975, the firm has grown into a global leader.
questions.explanation: 公司是「被創立」的，分詞構句用過去分詞 Founded。
questions.tip: Founded in + 年份 是公司介紹的固定開頭。
questions.sentenceZh: 這家公司創立於 1975 年，已成長為全球領導者。

questions.question: The manager, ______ with the results, extended the campaign.
questions.explanation: 人「感到滿意」用過去分詞 pleased（be pleased with）。
questions.tip: 人的情緒用 -ed：pleased、satisfied、delighted。
questions.sentenceZh: 經理對成果很滿意，於是延長了這檔活動。

questions.question: The decision to relocate ______ with mixed reactions from staff.
questions.explanation: be met with...（遭遇…的反應）是固定被動用法。
questions.tip: be met with + 反應（praise / criticism / mixed reactions）。
questions.sentenceZh: 搬遷的決定在員工之間引起了不同的反應。

questions.question: ______ the shipment arrived on time, we would not have lost the client.
questions.explanation: 與過去事實相反的假設倒裝：Had the shipment arrived = If the shipment had arrived。
questions.tip: 主句是 would have p.p. → 前面找 Had + S + p.p. 的倒裝。
questions.sentenceZh: 要是貨件準時到達，我們就不會失去那位客戶了。

questions.question: This time next week, the auditors ______ our financial records.
questions.explanation: This time next week（下週此刻）表示未來某時刻正在進行的動作，用未來進行式。
questions.tip: this time + 未來時間 → will be V-ing。
questions.sentenceZh: 下週的這個時候，稽核人員將正在審查我們的財務紀錄。

questions.question: The seminar was canceled ______ of low enrollment.
questions.explanation: on account of（由於）= because of；in charge of 是負責、on behalf of 是代表。
questions.tip: on account of = due to = because of。
questions.sentenceZh: 研討會因報名人數不足而取消。

questions.question: ______ the terms of the lease, tenants must give 60 days' notice.
questions.explanation: Under the terms of...（依據…的條款）是法律文件固定用語。
questions.tip: under the contract / terms / agreement = 依照合約。
questions.sentenceZh: 根據租約條款，房客必須提前六十天通知。

questions.question: The store is open daily ______ public holidays.
questions.explanation: 「每天營業，國定假日除外」用 except on（除了…之外）。其他選項都是「加上」的意思。
questions.tip: 營業時間公告常見：open daily except on holidays。
questions.sentenceZh: 這家店每天營業，國定假日除外。

questions.question: All expenses must be approved ______ writing by a supervisor.
questions.explanation: in writing（以書面形式）是固定搭配。
questions.tip: in writing、in person、in advance — in 的三個高頻片語。
questions.sentenceZh: 所有支出都必須經主管書面核准。

questions.question: The CEO attributed the growth ______ strong overseas sales.
questions.explanation: attribute A to B（把 A 歸因於 B）是固定搭配。
questions.tip: attribute / owe / dedicate + A + to + B。
questions.sentenceZh: 執行長將這波成長歸功於強勁的海外銷售。

questions.question: The consultant recommended that the company ______ its pricing model.
questions.explanation: recommend that + S + 原形動詞（假設語氣），should 被省略。
questions.tip: suggest / recommend / require / insist + that 子句 → 動詞用原形。
questions.sentenceZh: 顧問建議公司修改定價模式。

questions.question: It is essential that every visitor ______ a badge at all times.
questions.explanation: It is essential that + S + 原形動詞，即使主詞是第三人稱單數也不加 s。
questions.tip: It is essential / important / necessary that → 原形動詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 每位訪客務必隨時配戴識別證。

questions.question: The security system requires that all passwords ______ monthly.
questions.explanation: require that 子句用原形動詞，密碼是被更改的，故用 be changed。
questions.tip: require that + 被動 → be + p.p.（不是 are/is + p.p.）。
questions.sentenceZh: 安全系統要求所有密碼每月更換。

questions.question: We would rather ______ the meeting than cancel it entirely.
questions.explanation: would rather + 原形動詞 + than + 原形動詞。
questions.tip: would rather A than B，A、B 都用原形。
questions.sentenceZh: 我們寧願延期會議，也不願完全取消。

questions.question: The old printer needs ______ before the audit next week.
questions.explanation: need + V-ing 表被動（= need to be replaced）。
questions.tip: 物 + need + V-ing = 需要被…。
questions.sentenceZh: 這台舊印表機需要在下週稽核前汰換。

questions.question: The report contains a detailed ______ of last year's expenses.
questions.explanation: breakdown（明細；分項）符合「費用明細」的語意；breakthrough 是突破。
questions.tip: a breakdown of costs = 費用明細，多益報表題常客。
questions.sentenceZh: 這份報告包含去年支出的詳細明細。

questions.question: Passengers should keep their belongings in the overhead ______.
questions.explanation: overhead compartment（機艙上方置物櫃）是航空固定用語。
questions.tip: -partment 形近字：compartment 置物格、department 部門。
questions.sentenceZh: 乘客應將隨身物品放在頭頂置物櫃內。

questions.question: The company took immediate ______ to fix the security flaw.
questions.explanation: take measures（採取措施）是固定搭配。
questions.tip: take measures / steps / action 都是「採取行動」。
questions.sentenceZh: 公司立即採取措施修補安全漏洞。

questions.question: Ms. Alvarez has extensive ______ in international trade law.
questions.explanation: extensive expertise（豐富的專業知識）符合語意；expedition 是遠征。
questions.tip: expertise in + 領域 = 在…方面的專業。
questions.sentenceZh: Alvarez 在國際貿易法方面具備深厚的專業。

questions.question: The workshop gave participants ______ tips for managing time.
questions.explanation: practical tips（實用的訣竅）是自然搭配。
questions.tip: tips / advice 的常見修飾語：practical、useful、helpful。
questions.sentenceZh: 這場工作坊提供了學員實用的時間管理技巧。

questions.question: In ______ of your loyalty, we are pleased to offer you a special discount.
questions.explanation: in recognition of（為表彰…）是固定片語。
questions.tip: in recognition of your support / loyalty，感謝函高頻句。
questions.sentenceZh: 為感謝您的忠誠支持，我們很高興提供您一項特別折扣。

questions.question: The new policy will have a significant ______ on hiring practices.
questions.explanation: have an impact on（對…產生影響）是固定搭配；impress 是動詞。
questions.tip: impact / effect / influence + on。
questions.sentenceZh: 新政策將對聘僱作業產生重大影響。

questions.question: Employees are reminded to use the office supplies ______.
questions.explanation: sparingly（節省地）符合「提醒節約使用文具」的語意。
questions.tip: use sparingly = 省著用，公告常見。
questions.sentenceZh: 提醒員工節約使用辦公用品。

questions.question: The manual provides step-by-step ______ for installing the printer.
questions.explanation: step-by-step instructions（逐步指示）是固定搭配。
questions.tip: instructions 說明、instruments 儀器、institutions 機構。
questions.sentenceZh: 手冊提供了安裝印表機的逐步說明。

questions.question: The board expressed ______ about the rising production costs.
questions.explanation: express concern about（對…表達憂慮）是固定搭配；consent 是同意。
questions.tip: concern about / over = 對…的擔憂。
questions.sentenceZh: 董事會對不斷上升的生產成本表示憂心。

questions.question: The Tokyo branch outperformed ______ in Seoul this quarter.
questions.explanation: that 代替前面的單數名詞 the branch，指「首爾的那間分店」。
questions.tip: 比較句中代替前述名詞：單數用 that、複數用 those。
questions.sentenceZh: 東京分公司本季表現優於首爾分公司。

questions.question: Employees who complete the survey will have ______ names entered into a drawing.
questions.explanation: 空格後接名詞 names，用所有格 their。
questions.tip: 空格 + 名詞 → 所有格。
questions.sentenceZh: 完成問卷的員工將獲得抽獎資格。

questions.question: The job involves answering calls, scheduling meetings, and ______ reports.
questions.explanation: 並列結構要一致：answering、scheduling、filing 都是動名詞。
questions.tip: A, B, and C 三者詞性必須平行。
questions.sentenceZh: 這份工作包含接聽電話、安排會議和歸檔報告。

questions.question: By the time you receive this letter, I ______ for Singapore.
questions.explanation: By the time + 現在式子句，主句用未來完成式 will have left。
questions.tip: by the time + 現在式 → 主句 will have p.p.。
questions.sentenceZh: 當你收到這封信時，我將已啟程前往新加坡。

questions.question: The number of online orders ______ steadily since the app was released.
questions.explanation: The number of + 複數名詞 是單數主詞，且 since 搭配完成式，用 has increased。
questions.tip: the number of（數量）單數；a number of（許多）複數。
questions.sentenceZh: 自從 App 上線以來，線上訂單數量持續穩定成長。

questions.question: Neither of the printers ______ working properly.
questions.explanation: Neither of + 複數名詞 作主詞時，動詞用單數 is。
questions.tip: neither / each / either of + 複數名詞 → 單數動詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 這兩台印表機都無法正常運作。

questions.question: Each of the candidates ______ asked to prepare a short presentation.
questions.explanation: Each of + 複數名詞 是單數主詞，用 was。
questions.tip: each 永遠搭配單數動詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 每位候選人都被要求準備一段簡短的簡報。

questions.question: The launch was a success, ______ initial concerns about the budget.
questions.explanation: 空格後接名詞片語，需要介系詞 notwithstanding（儘管）；nevertheless / however 是副詞，不能直接接名詞。
questions.tip: notwithstanding + 名詞 = despite。
questions.sentenceZh: 儘管起初對預算有所疑慮，這次上市仍然成功。

questions.question: ______ demand remains high, we will keep the extended hours.
questions.explanation: As long as（只要）表條件，符合「只要需求維持就延長營業」的語意。
questions.tip: as long as 只要；as soon as 一…就；as far as 就…而言。
questions.sentenceZh: 只要需求維持強勁，我們就會延續延長營業時間。

questions.question: The flight was overbooked; ______, several passengers volunteered to take a later one.
questions.explanation: 超賣（原因）→ 乘客自願改搭（結果），因果關係用 consequently。
questions.tip: 分號後的副詞看前後句關係：因果 → consequently / as a result。
questions.sentenceZh: 班機超賣，因此數名乘客自願改搭較晚的航班。

questions.question:
Dear Ms. Okafor,

Thank you for applying for the marketing coordinator position. We were impressed by your qualifications. ______, we have decided to move forward with another candidate whose experience more closely matches our needs.
questions.explanation: 前句稱讚資歷，後句是婉拒（負面消息），語氣轉折用 Regrettably（遺憾地）。
questions.tip: 婉拒信的轉折詞：regrettably / unfortunately / however。
questions.sentenceZh: 很遺憾，我們決定錄取另一位經歷更符合需求的候選人。

questions.question: Attention all staff: The parking garage will be repainted this weekend. ______ your vehicle from Level 2 by Friday evening.
questions.explanation: 公告中的指示句用祈使句：Please remove...。
questions.tip: 公告要求讀者做某事 → Please + 原形動詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 請在週五晚間前將您的車輛移出二樓停車場。

questions.question: Our records show that your subscription will expire on August 15. Renew before that date to enjoy uninterrupted service. ______, your account will be suspended automatically.
questions.explanation: 「若不續約，帳戶將自動停用」，表示「否則」用 Otherwise。
questions.tip: 前句是建議、後句是不照做的後果 → otherwise。
questions.sentenceZh: 否則，您的帳戶將被自動停權。

questions.question: The community center offers free yoga classes every Saturday. Spaces are limited to twenty participants per session. ______ early to secure your spot.
questions.explanation: 指示讀者的祈使句用原形動詞 Arrive。
questions.tip: 句首動詞 + to V 目的 → 祈使句用原形。
questions.sentenceZh: 請提早到場以確保您的位置。

questions.question: We are pleased to announce that GreenLeaf Catering has won the contract for the cafeteria. ______ September 1, they will provide all on-site meals.
questions.explanation: Effective + 日期（自…起生效）是公告固定用語。
questions.tip: Effective March 1 = 自三月一日起。
questions.sentenceZh: 自 9 月 1 日起，他們將供應所有的現場餐點。

questions.question: Thank you for your purchase. If you are not completely happy with the product, you may return it within 30 days. A full refund will be issued once we ______ the returned item.
questions.explanation: once（一旦）引導的時間子句用現在式代替未來式。
questions.tip: when / once / after 子句：用現在式表未來。
questions.sentenceZh: 收到退回商品後，我們將全額退款。

questions.question: The IT department will upgrade all office computers next week. Employees are asked to back up important files in advance. Any files ______ on the desktop may be lost during the upgrade.
questions.explanation: 檔案是「被留下」的，用過去分詞 left 修飾 files（= files that are left）。
questions.tip: 名詞 + p.p. = 被…的名詞（縮減關係子句）。
questions.sentenceZh: 留在桌面上的任何檔案都可能在升級過程中遺失。

questions.question: Maple Grove Apartments is pleased to welcome new tenants. Please note that the fitness room is for residents only. Guests may use the pool ______ accompanied by a resident.
questions.explanation: 「賓客在住戶陪同時可使用泳池」，用 when（= when they are accompanied）。
questions.tip: when / while + p.p. 是省略主詞 be 的常見結構。
questions.sentenceZh: 住戶陪同時，訪客可以使用游泳池。

questions.question: The annual charity run will take place on Sunday, October 12. More than a thousand runners are expected to ______ in this year's event.
questions.explanation: be expected to + 原形動詞，故填 participate。
questions.tip: to 後面接原形動詞（除非 to 是介系詞）。
questions.sentenceZh: 預計將有超過一千名跑者參加今年的賽事。

questions.question: To all conference attendees: Presentation slides will be available for download after the event. ______ can be found on the conference website under the 'Resources' tab.
questions.explanation: 指前句複數的 slides，作主詞用 They。
questions.tip: 先確認指涉對象的單複數與格位：主詞位置用 they。
questions.sentenceZh: 這些資料可以在大會網站的「Resources」頁籤下找到。

questions.question: All visitors must sign in at the front desk upon ______ at the headquarters.
questions.explanation: 介系詞 upon 後面要接名詞，upon arrival（抵達時）是固定用法。arrive 是動詞原形、arrived 是過去式；arriving 雖是動名詞，但 upon arrival 為最常見的慣用搭配。
questions.tip: upon / on + arrival（一抵達就……）是多益高頻固定用法，直接背起來。
questions.sentenceZh: 所有訪客抵達總部時，必須在前台簽到。

questions.question: The marketing team made a ______ decision to delay the product launch.
questions.explanation: 空格修飾名詞 decision，需要形容詞 strategic（策略性的）。strategy 是名詞、strategically 是副詞、strategize 是動詞。
questions.tip: 冠詞 a 與名詞之間的空格，九成填形容詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 行銷團隊做出了延後產品上市的策略性決定。

questions.question: Employees who complete the training program ______ receive a certificate.
questions.explanation: 空格位於助動詞省略的主詞與動詞 receive 之間，修飾動詞需要副詞 successfully。句意：成功完成訓練課程的員工將獲得證書。
questions.tip: 主詞與動詞之間的空格，優先考慮副詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 順利完成培訓課程的員工將獲得證書。

questions.question: The hotel is known for its ______ to customer satisfaction.
questions.explanation: 所有格 its 後面接名詞，commitment to（對……的投入／承諾）為固定搭配。commit 是動詞，committed 是過去分詞或形容詞。
questions.tip: 所有格（its / their / our）後面必接名詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 這家飯店以致力於顧客滿意而聞名。

questions.question: Ms. Rivera responded ______ to the client's urgent request.
questions.explanation: 修飾動詞 responded 需要副詞 promptly（迅速地）。prompt 是形容詞、promptness 是名詞。
questions.tip: 不及物動詞後面的空格常填副詞：respond promptly、rise sharply。
questions.sentenceZh: Rivera 迅速回應了客戶的緊急請求。

questions.question: The ______ of the new branch office has been postponed until next spring.
questions.explanation: 定冠詞 the 與 of 之間需要名詞，opening（開幕）作名詞用。openly 是副詞。
questions.tip: 「the ______ of」結構填名詞；動詞加 -ing 常可當名詞（opening、planning）。
questions.sentenceZh: 新分公司的開幕已延後至明年春天。

questions.question: Please handle the glassware with extreme ______ during shipping.
questions.explanation: 形容詞 extreme 修飾的對象需要名詞，with care（小心地）為固定用法。careful 是形容詞、carefully 是副詞。
questions.tip: with + 形容詞 + 名詞（with extreme care / with great caution）。
questions.sentenceZh: 運送期間請極度小心地搬運玻璃器皿。

questions.question: The board expressed strong ______ for the proposed merger.
questions.explanation: 形容詞 strong 後面接名詞，句意需要抽象名詞 support（支持）。supporter 是「支持者」，語意不符。
questions.tip: 同是名詞也要選對語意：support（支持）vs. supporter（支持者）。
questions.sentenceZh: 董事會對這項合併提案表達了強力支持。

questions.question: The company offers a full ______ if customers are not satisfied with their purchase.
questions.explanation: 句意：若顧客不滿意，公司提供全額退款（refund）。receipt 是收據、revenue 是營收、reduction 是減少。
questions.tip: a full refund（全額退款）是客服情境高頻搭配。
questions.sentenceZh: 如果顧客對購買的商品不滿意，公司提供全額退款。

questions.question: Due to rising material costs, the manufacturer decided to ______ prices by five percent.
questions.explanation: raise 是及物動詞（提高某物），後面接受詞 prices。rise 和 arise 是不及物動詞，不能接受詞。
questions.tip: raise + 受詞（及物）；rise 自己上升（不及物）。看空格後有無受詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 由於原料成本上漲，製造商決定調漲百分之五的價格。

questions.question: All laboratory staff must ______ with the updated safety regulations.
questions.explanation: 空格後有介系詞 with，只有 comply with（遵守）需要搭配 with。obey、follow、observe 都是及物動詞，直接接受詞。
questions.tip: 先看空格後的介系詞再選字：comply with、adhere to、abide by。
questions.sentenceZh: 所有實驗室人員都必須遵守更新後的安全規範。

questions.question: The seminar will provide ______ information about overseas investment opportunities.
questions.explanation: 句意：研討會將提供關於海外投資機會的寶貴資訊（valuable information）。spacious 形容空間寬敞、grateful 是感激的、eventual 是最終的。
questions.tip: 形容詞字彙題先確認被修飾的名詞：valuable information 是常見搭配。
questions.sentenceZh: 這場研討會將提供關於海外投資機會的寶貴資訊。

questions.question: Ticket holders may ______ their seats up to 24 hours before the performance.
questions.explanation: 句意：持票者可在演出前 24 小時內更換座位（exchange seats）。expend 是花費、exclaim 是驚呼、exceed 是超過。
questions.tip: 字首相同的選項是經典陷阱，逐一帶入句意檢查。
questions.sentenceZh: 持票者最遲可在演出前 24 小時更換座位。

questions.question: The new policy will take ______ on the first day of next month.
questions.explanation: take effect（生效）是固定片語。affect 是動詞（影響）、effort 是努力、efficiency 是效率。
questions.tip: take effect（生效）、in effect（生效中）、effective + 日期（自某日起生效）。
questions.sentenceZh: 新政策將於下個月一號生效。

questions.question: Applicants must submit two letters of ______ along with their resumes.
questions.explanation: 求職情境：履歷須附兩封推薦信（letters of recommendation）。recognition 是表彰、reservation 是預約、renovation 是翻修。
questions.tip: letter of recommendation（推薦信）是人事招募高頻詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 申請者必須連同履歷繳交兩封推薦信。

questions.question: The finance department is responsible for ______ the annual budget report.
questions.explanation: 句意：財務部負責編製年度預算報告（prepare the report）。repair 是修理、compare 是比較、declare 是申報／宣布，語意皆不符。
questions.tip: prepare a report / budget / presentation 是辦公室高頻搭配。
questions.sentenceZh: 財務部門負責編製年度預算報告。

questions.question: Despite the heavy rain, the outdoor concert proceeded as ______.
questions.explanation: as scheduled（照原定計畫）是固定用法。句意：儘管大雨，戶外音樂會仍照常舉行。
questions.tip: as scheduled / as planned / as expected（照計畫／如預期）成組記。
questions.sentenceZh: 儘管下大雨，戶外音樂會仍照原定計畫進行。

questions.question: The quarterly sales report is due ______ the end of this week.
questions.explanation: by + 時間點表示「最遲在……之前完成」，搭配 due 表期限。until 強調動作持續到某時間點，與 due 不搭。
questions.tip: 期限用 by（一次性完成）；持續狀態才用 until。
questions.sentenceZh: 季度銷售報告必須在本週結束前繳交。

questions.question: ______ its compact size, the new printer can handle large-volume jobs.
questions.explanation: 空格後是名詞片語 its compact size，要用介系詞 Despite（儘管）。Although 和 While 是連接詞，後面須接完整子句。
questions.tip: 後接名詞選 Despite / In spite of；後接子句選 Although / Though。
questions.sentenceZh: 儘管體積小巧，這台新印表機仍能處理大量列印工作。

questions.question: The two companies have been working together ______ more than a decade.
questions.explanation: for + 一段時間（more than a decade），搭配現在完成進行式。since 後接時間起點、during 後接特定期間名詞。
questions.tip: for + 時間長度；since + 起始時間點。
questions.sentenceZh: 這兩家公司已合作超過十年。

questions.question: Please refer ______ the user manual before contacting technical support.
questions.explanation: refer to（參閱）是固定搭配：聯絡技術支援前請先參閱使用手冊。
questions.tip: refer to / respond to / subscribe to，動詞固定搭配 to 的一組。
questions.sentenceZh: 聯絡技術支援之前，請先參閱使用手冊。

questions.question: The museum is conveniently located ______ walking distance of the train station.
questions.explanation: within walking distance of（步行可達範圍內）是固定用法。
questions.tip: within walking distance / within budget / within 30 days，within 表「在範圍內」。
questions.sentenceZh: 博物館位置便利，距離火車站步行可達。

questions.question: All complaints should be directed ______ the customer service manager.
questions.explanation: direct A to B（把 A 轉給 B）：所有客訴應轉給客服經理，被動式 be directed to。
questions.tip: 轉交、指向類動詞（direct / forward / address）搭配 to。
questions.sentenceZh: 所有投訴應轉交給客服經理處理。

questions.question: By the time the auditors arrive next Monday, the accounting team ______ all the financial records.
questions.explanation: by the time + 未來時間點，主要子句用未來完成式 will have organized，表示屆時已完成。
questions.tip: by the time + 未來時間，搭配未來完成式（will have + p.p.）。
questions.sentenceZh: 等到稽核人員下週一抵達時，會計團隊將已整理好所有財務紀錄。

questions.question: Mr. Tanaka ______ for the company since it was founded in 2010.
questions.explanation: since + 過去時間點（2010 年創立），主要子句用現在完成式 has worked，表示從過去持續到現在。
questions.tip: 看到 since + 過去時間，主句選現在完成式。
questions.sentenceZh: Tanaka 自公司 2010 年創立以來便一直在此工作。

questions.question: The maintenance crew ______ the elevator when the power outage occurred.
questions.explanation: when 引導的過去事件（停電）發生時，另一動作正在進行，用過去進行式 was inspecting。
questions.tip: 過去進行式 + when + 過去簡單式：長動作被短事件打斷。
questions.sentenceZh: 停電發生時，維修人員正在檢查電梯。

questions.question: Once the contract ______, we will begin production immediately.
questions.explanation: once / when / as soon as 引導的時間子句用現在式代替未來式，且合約是被簽，用被動 is signed。
questions.tip: 時間、條件子句不用未來式：Once it is signed（不是 will be signed）。
questions.sentenceZh: 合約一經簽署，我們就會立即開始生產。

questions.question: The IT department ______ the new security system at the end of last quarter.
questions.explanation: at the end of last quarter 是明確的過去時間，用過去簡單式 installed。有明確過去時間點時不能用現在完成式。
questions.tip: 句尾有 last / ago / yesterday 等過去時間，直接選過去簡單式。
questions.sentenceZh: 資訊部門在上季末安裝了新的保全系統。

questions.question: The committee recommended ______ the deadline for grant applications.
questions.explanation: recommend 後面接動名詞 V-ing：建議延長申請期限。recommend / suggest / consider / avoid 都接動名詞。
questions.tip: 「建議考慮避免享受完成」類動詞（recommend / consider / avoid / enjoy / finish）接 V-ing。
questions.sentenceZh: 委員會建議延長補助申請的截止日期。

questions.question: We are pleased ______ that your application has been approved.
questions.explanation: be pleased to + 原形動詞（很高興通知您……）是商業書信固定開頭。
questions.tip: be pleased / delighted / happy to V：書信高頻句型。
questions.sentenceZh: 我們很高興通知您，您的申請已獲核准。

questions.question: The store manager had the shelves ______ before the holiday sale began.
questions.explanation: have + 受詞 + 過去分詞：貨架「被補貨」，受詞與動詞是被動關係，用 restocked。
questions.tip: have + 物 + p.p.（請人做某事）；have + 人 + 原形（叫某人做）。
questions.sentenceZh: 店長在假期特賣開始前請人補齊了貨架。

questions.question: To avoid ______ extra fees, please return the rental car with a full tank.
questions.explanation: avoid 後面接動名詞 paying：為避免支付額外費用，還車時請加滿油。
questions.tip: avoid + V-ing 固定搭配，多益租車、飯店情境常見。
questions.sentenceZh: 為避免支付額外費用，請將租賃車輛加滿油後歸還。

questions.question: The security guard asked all visitors ______ their identification badges at all times.
questions.explanation: ask + 受詞 + to V：要求訪客隨時配戴識別證。
questions.tip: ask / require / advise / encourage + 人 + to V。
questions.sentenceZh: 警衛要求所有訪客隨時配戴識別證。

questions.question: ______ the flight was delayed, Ms. Chen still arrived in time for the opening ceremony.
questions.explanation: 空格後是完整子句（the flight was delayed），要用連接詞 Although。Despite 和 Because of 是介系詞，後接名詞。
questions.tip: 後面是「主詞＋動詞」選連接詞；只有名詞選介系詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 雖然班機延誤，Chen 女士仍及時趕上開幕典禮。

questions.question: Please save your work frequently ______ you lose important data during a power failure.
questions.explanation: in case（以防萬一）：請經常存檔，以防停電時遺失重要資料。so that 表目的（為了能夠）、now that 表既然。
questions.tip: in case + 子句 = 以防……發生；lest / for fear that 同義。
questions.sentenceZh: 請經常儲存您的工作，以免停電時遺失重要資料。

questions.question: You may pay by credit card ______ in cash at any of our branches.
questions.explanation: either A or B 的省略形：可用信用卡或現金付款，兩個選項並列用 or。
questions.tip: 並列選擇用 or；nor 用於否定並列（neither...nor）。
questions.sentenceZh: 您可以在我們任何分行以信用卡或現金付款。

questions.question: ______ you have any questions about your invoice, please contact our billing department.
questions.explanation: Should you have... 是 If you should have... 的倒裝省略，商業書信常用：如有任何帳單問題，請聯絡帳務部門。
questions.tip: 句首 Should + 主詞 + 原形動詞 = If 條件句的正式倒裝。
questions.sentenceZh: 若您對帳單有任何疑問，請聯絡我們的帳務部門。

questions.question: All packages ______ before they leave the distribution center.
questions.explanation: 包裹是「被檢查」，用被動語態 are inspected。主詞 packages 無法自己執行檢查動作。
questions.tip: 主詞是物、動作由人執行時，多半用被動。
questions.sentenceZh: 所有包裹離開配送中心前都會經過檢查。

questions.question: The award ceremony ______ in the grand ballroom next Friday evening.
questions.explanation: hold（舉行）的主詞是典禮本身時用被動：典禮將於下週五晚間在大宴會廳舉行。
questions.tip: be held（被舉行）：meeting / ceremony / event 當主詞時幾乎都用被動。
questions.sentenceZh: 頒獎典禮將於下週五晚間在大宴會廳舉行。

questions.question: Employees ______ to update their passwords every ninety days.
questions.explanation: be required to V（被要求做某事）：員工每九十天須更新密碼。員工是被要求的一方，用被動。
questions.tip: be required / expected / encouraged to V：規定類句型固定用被動。
questions.sentenceZh: 員工必須每九十天更新一次密碼。

questions.question: The results of the customer survey were quite ______ to the product development team.
questions.explanation: 調查結果「令人驚訝」，修飾事物用現在分詞 surprising。surprised 用於形容人的感受。
questions.tip: 事物 + -ing（令人……）；人 + -ed（感到……）。
questions.sentenceZh: 顧客調查的結果讓產品開發團隊相當意外。

questions.question: ______ in 1985, the firm has grown into one of the region's largest law practices.
questions.explanation: 分詞構句：公司是「被創立」，用過去分詞 Founded。完整句為 Since it was founded in 1985...。
questions.tip: 句首分詞看主詞關係：主詞被動作影響用 p.p.，主詞主動執行用 V-ing。
questions.sentenceZh: 這家事務所創立於 1985 年，已成長為該地區最大的法律事務所之一。

questions.question: Passengers ______ connecting flights should proceed directly to Terminal 2.
questions.explanation: 分詞片語修飾 passengers：乘客主動「搭乘」轉機航班，用現在分詞 taking（= who are taking）。
questions.tip: 名詞後的分詞 = 省略關代的形容詞子句：主動 V-ing、被動 p.p.。
questions.sentenceZh: 轉機的乘客請直接前往第二航廈。

questions.question: If the shipment had arrived on time, we ______ the order by now.
questions.explanation: 與過去事實相反的假設：If + had p.p.，主要子句用 would have p.p.。貨若準時到，訂單現在早就完成了。
questions.tip: If had p.p. 配 would have p.p.，第三條件句成對出現。
questions.sentenceZh: 如果貨件當時準時到達，我們現在早就完成訂單了。

questions.question: The director requested that the budget proposal ______ by Friday afternoon.
questions.explanation: request / suggest / insist + that 子句用原形動詞（should 省略），且提案是被提交，用 be submitted。
questions.tip: 要求建議類動詞 + that + 主詞 + (should) + 原形動詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 總監要求預算提案在週五下午前提交。

questions.question: ______ it not for the generous sponsorship, the charity event could not take place.
questions.explanation: Were it not for...（若非……）是 If it were not for 的倒裝，表與現在事實相反：若非慷慨贊助，慈善活動無法舉辦。
questions.tip: Were it not for（現在）／ Had it not been for（過去）：「若非」的正式倒裝。
questions.sentenceZh: 若非這筆慷慨的贊助，慈善活動根本無法舉行。

questions.question: The consultant ______ report impressed the board will lead the new project.
questions.explanation: 空格後是名詞 report，表示「顧問的報告」，用所有格關係代名詞 whose。
questions.tip: 關代後面直接接名詞，選 whose。
questions.sentenceZh: 報告令董事會印象深刻的那位顧問將主導新專案。

questions.question: The workshop, ______ was originally planned for March, has been moved to May.
questions.explanation: 逗號後的非限定關係子句只能用 which，不能用 that。先行詞 workshop 是事物。
questions.tip: 逗號 + 關代 = 非限定用法，that 出局。
questions.sentenceZh: 原訂三月舉行的工作坊已改到五月。

questions.question: This year's trade fair attracted ______ visitors than any previous event.
questions.explanation: 有 than，用比較級 more。visitors 是可數複數，more 修飾可數與不可數皆可。
questions.tip: 看到 than 鎖定比較級；比較對象 any previous event 排除最高級。
questions.sentenceZh: 今年的商展吸引了比以往任何一屆都多的參觀者。

questions.question: Of all the applicants we interviewed, Ms. Park is the ______ qualified for the position.
questions.explanation: of all...（在所有……之中）搭配最高級 the most qualified。
questions.tip: of all / among + 複數 = 最高級信號。
questions.sentenceZh: Park 是這個職位最適任的人選。

questions.question: Employees may pick up ______ parking permits at the security office.
questions.explanation: 空格後有名詞 parking permits，需要所有格 their。theirs 是所有格代名詞，後面不接名詞。
questions.tip: 後面有名詞用 their；沒有名詞才用 theirs。
questions.sentenceZh: 員工可以到警衛室領取停車證。

questions.question: ______ of the two proposals has its own advantages and drawbacks.
questions.explanation: 空格後的動詞 has 是單數，Both / All / Most 都接複數動詞，只有 Each（每一個）接單數動詞。
questions.tip: Each / Every + 單數動詞；Both / All + 複數動詞。先看動詞單複數。
questions.sentenceZh: 這兩份提案各有優缺點。

questions.question: In ______ to your inquiry, we have attached our latest product catalog.
questions.explanation: in response to（回覆……）是商業書信固定片語：茲回覆您的詢問，附上最新產品目錄。
questions.tip: in response to / in reference to / in regard to：書信開頭三兄弟。
questions.sentenceZh: 回覆您的詢問，我們附上了最新的產品型錄。

questions.question: ______ has the company received so many positive reviews in a single quarter.
questions.explanation: 否定副詞 Never before 置於句首引起倒裝（has the company received）。句意：公司從未在單季收到這麼多好評。
questions.tip: 句首否定詞（Never / Rarely / Seldom）+ 助動詞 + 主詞 = 倒裝。
questions.sentenceZh: 公司從未在單一季度收到過這麼多好評。

questions.question: The new intern is responsible for answering phones, filing documents, and ______ meeting rooms.
questions.explanation: and 連接的三個項目須平行：answering、filing、preparing 都用動名詞。
questions.tip: A, B, and C 三項結構，詞性必須一致。
questions.sentenceZh: 新來的實習生負責接電話、歸檔文件和佈置會議室。

questions.question: The venue was fully booked; ______, we decided to postpone the banquet until next month.
questions.explanation: 前後為因果關係：場地已訂滿，因此決定延期。otherwise 表否則、nevertheless 表然而，皆不符。
questions.tip: 分號後的轉承副詞看邏輯：因果 therefore、轉折 however、否則 otherwise。
questions.sentenceZh: 場地已被訂滿，因此我們決定將晚宴延到下個月。

questions.question: Dear Valued Customer: Thank you for shopping with GreenLeaf Home. Your order #4382 has been shipped. ______ your tracking number below to follow the delivery status online.
questions.explanation: 此句為祈使句，指示顧客採取行動，用原形動詞 Use 開頭。
questions.tip: 通知信中的指示句常用祈使句：Please use / Click / Visit。
questions.sentenceZh: 請使用下方的追蹤號碼在線上查詢配送狀態。

questions.question: Notice to all tenants: The building's water supply will be shut off on Saturday from 9 A.M. to 1 P.M. for pipe maintenance. We apologize for any ______ this may cause.
questions.explanation: 公告道歉的固定句型：We apologize for any inconvenience this may cause（造成不便，敬請見諒）。
questions.tip: apologize for any inconvenience 整句背起來，公告題必考。
questions.sentenceZh: 對於由此造成的任何不便，我們深感抱歉。

questions.question: MEMO: Starting next month, the cafeteria will extend its operating hours until 8 P.M. ______, a wider selection of healthy meal options will be introduced.
questions.explanation: 前句延長營業時間、後句增加健康餐點，兩者為並列的新措施，用 In addition（此外）。
questions.tip: 篇章填空轉承詞看前後句關係：並列補充用 In addition / Moreover。
questions.sentenceZh: 此外，將引進更多樣的健康餐點選擇。

questions.question: Job Posting: Riverside Hotel is seeking a front desk associate. Candidates should have at least one year of hospitality experience. ______ who pass the initial screening will be invited to an interview next week.
questions.explanation: 空格後的動詞 pass 與 will be invited 均為複數搭配，Those who（通過初選者）為正解。Anyone / Whoever / Each 接單數動詞。
questions.tip: Those who + 複數動詞（……的人們）；anyone who + 單數動詞。
questions.sentenceZh: 通過初審者將受邀參加下週的面試。

passages.label: 公告 (Notice)
passages.text:
NOTICE TO ALL TENANTS — Harborview Office Tower

The main elevators will be out of service for scheduled maintenance from Friday, March 14, at 8:00 P.M. until Monday, March 17, at 6:00 A.M. During this period, please use the freight elevator located next to the loading dock or the stairways at either end of the building.

Tenants expecting weekend deliveries should notify the security desk in advance at extension 210 so that access to the freight elevator can be arranged.

We apologize for any inconvenience.

Building Management Office

questions.question: What is the purpose of the notice?
options: To announce a temporary elevator shutdown
To introduce a new security procedure
To report a change in business hours
questions.explanation: 公告開頭即說明主電梯將於 3/14 至 3/17 因排定保養停止服務，故目的為宣布電梯暫停使用。
questions.tip: 目的題（purpose）看首段第一、二句即可鎖定答案，不必精讀全文。

questions.question: What are tenants asked to do before receiving weekend deliveries?
questions.explanation: 文中要求「notify the security desk in advance at extension 210」，即事先聯絡警衛櫃台，以安排貨梯使用。
questions.tip: 細節題先抓題目關鍵字（weekend deliveries），回文章定位該句，答案通常在同一句或下一句。

passages.label: 簡訊串 (Text-message chain)
passages.text:
Nadia Osei (10:12 A.M.)
Hi Tom, the courier just called. Our booth materials for the trade show won't arrive until Thursday afternoon.

Tom Reyes (10:14 A.M.)
That's cutting it close. Setup has to be finished by Thursday at 9 P.M.

Nadia Osei (10:15 A.M.)
I know. Should we ask Ken and Priya to stay late and help us?

Tom Reyes (10:17 A.M.)
Already ahead of you. They both said yes. I'll book a van so we can move everything in one trip.

Nadia Osei (10:18 A.M.)
Perfect. I'll print the product brochures here so at least those are ready.

questions.question: Why is Ms. Osei concerned?
options: Some materials will arrive later than expected
questions.explanation: Nadia 第一則訊息說攤位物料要到週四下午才會送達，而佈置必須在週四晚上九點前完成，時間緊迫。
questions.tip: 簡訊題先看第一、二則訊息，通常就點出整段對話的問題核心。

questions.question: At 10:17 A.M., what does Mr. Reyes most likely mean when he writes, "Already ahead of you"?
options: He has arrived at the trade show first.
He has already asked the colleagues for help.
He has finished printing the brochures.
questions.explanation: Nadia 才剛提議請 Ken 和 Priya 幫忙，Tom 回覆「我早一步了，他們都答應了」，表示他已先詢問過兩位同事。
questions.tip: 「引述句意圖」題必考：答案永遠取決於前一則訊息的語境，不要只看字面意思。

passages.label: 廣告 (Advertisement)
passages.text:
GRAND OPENING — RIVERSTONE FITNESS CENTER

Join during the month of April and pay no enrollment fee!

Riverstone Fitness Center offers state-of-the-art exercise equipment, an indoor swimming pool, and more than thirty group classes each week, including yoga, spinning, and boxing. Our certified trainers are available for one-on-one sessions at an additional cost.

Membership options:
- Basic (gym access only): $35 per month
- Plus (gym and pool): $50 per month
- Premium (all facilities and unlimited classes): $65 per month

All new members receive a complimentary fitness assessment. Corporate discounts are available for companies that register ten or more employees.

Visit www.riverstonefit.com or call 555-0188.

questions.question: What is offered to customers who join in April?
questions.explanation: 廣告第一句「Join during the month of April and pay no enrollment fee!」，四月入會免入會費。
questions.tip: 廣告題型的優惠條件常放在標題正下方第一句，先讀那裡。

questions.question: How much is a monthly membership that includes pool access but not group classes?
questions.explanation: Plus 方案（gym and pool）為每月 $50；Premium 才包含無限團體課程。
questions.tip: 價目表題請逐項比對條件（含什麼、不含什麼），不要選最貴或最便宜的直覺答案。

questions.question: According to the advertisement, who can receive a corporate discount?
options: Members who sign a one-year contract
Companies registering at least ten employees
questions.explanation: 文末說明「Corporate discounts are available for companies that register ten or more employees」，即登記十名以上員工的公司。
questions.tip: 數字條件（ten or more）常被改寫成 at least ten，注意同義改寫。

passages.label: 電子郵件 (E-mail)
passages.text:
To: Amelia Chen <a.chen@fastmail.com>
From: Recruitment Team <careers@nortonlogistics.com>
Date: June 2
Subject: Interview Invitation — Operations Analyst

Dear Ms. Chen,

Thank you for applying for the Operations Analyst position at Norton Logistics. We were impressed by your background in supply chain management, and we would like to invite you to an interview at our headquarters on Tuesday, June 10, at 2:00 P.M.

The interview will last approximately one hour and will include a short data analysis exercise. Please bring one form of photo identification and a printed copy of your résumé. Parking is available in the visitor lot on Dunham Street; be sure to register your license plate number at the front desk upon arrival.

If the proposed time is inconvenient, please reply to this e-mail by Friday, June 6, with two alternative times.

We look forward to meeting you.

Sincerely,
Recruitment Team, Norton Logistics

questions.question: Why was the e-mail sent to Ms. Chen?
questions.explanation: 郵件主旨與首段皆表明邀請 Ms. Chen 於 6/10 下午兩點到總部面試，屬面試邀約，尚未錄取（不是 offer a job）。
questions.tip: 「Why was the e-mail sent」= 主旨題，看 Subject 行加首段即可，別被結尾客套話誤導。

questions.question: What is Ms. Chen asked to bring to the interview?
options: A photo ID and a résumé
questions.explanation: 第二段明確要求攜帶「one form of photo identification and a printed copy of your résumé」。
questions.tip: bring / required documents 是常見細節題，通常集中在中段的指示句。

questions.question: What should Ms. Chen do if she cannot attend on June 10?
options: Reply with other available times by June 6
questions.explanation: 末段指示：若時間不便，須在 6/6（週五）前回信提供兩個替代時段。
questions.tip: 條件句（If...）幾乎必出題，看到 if 就先畫線。

passages.label: 備忘錄 (Memo)
passages.text:
MEMO

To: All Department Heads
From: Dana Whitfield, Chief Financial Officer
Date: September 1
Re: New travel expense procedure

Effective October 1, all business travel expenses must be submitted through the new SwiftClaim online system. Paper reimbursement forms will no longer be accepted after that date.

To use the system, employees should scan their receipts and upload them within fourteen days of returning from a trip. Claims submitted after this period will require written approval from a department head. Meal expenses will be reimbursed up to $60 per day; receipts are required for any single purchase over $25.

A thirty-minute training webinar will be held on September 18 and again on September 24. Attendance at one session is mandatory for all staff who travel on company business.

Questions may be directed to the finance help desk at helpdesk@corfin.com.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the memo?
options: To announce a change in expense reporting
To introduce a new department head
questions.explanation: 備忘錄宣布 10/1 起差旅費用改用 SwiftClaim 線上系統申報、紙本表單停用，即申報流程變更。
questions.tip: Re:（主旨）行常直接洩題——「New travel expense procedure」就是答案方向。

questions.question: What happens if a claim is submitted more than fourteen days after a trip?
options: It needs a department head's written approval.
It must be resubmitted on paper.
questions.explanation: 文中說明逾期申報「will require written approval from a department head」，需要部門主管書面核准，並非直接拒絕。
questions.tip: 注意選項的「程度」：文章說需要核准，不等於自動退件，別選過度延伸的選項。

questions.question: What are traveling employees required to do?
options: Book flights through the finance office
questions.explanation: 第三段：訓練網路研討會辦兩場，出差員工「Attendance at one session is mandatory」，必須參加其中一場。
questions.tip: mandatory / required / must 出現處必是考點，先圈起來。

passages.label: 文章 (Article)
passages.text:
Local Roastery Expands Overseas

MILLBROOK (May 3) — Cedar & Bean, the coffee roastery founded eight years ago in a converted warehouse on Quarry Road, announced yesterday that it will open its first international café in Osaka, Japan, this autumn.

The company, which began by supplying beans to local restaurants, now operates six cafés across the region and employs more than 120 people. Founder Marisol Vega credits the firm's growth to its direct partnerships with small farms. "We pay growers above-market rates, and in return we get beans nobody else can offer," she said.

The Osaka location will be managed by Kenji Sato, who joined Cedar & Bean three years ago after running his own tea shop. The company plans to hire fifteen local employees before the opening.

Ms. Vega added that if the Osaka café performs well, two more Asian locations could follow within two years.

questions.question: What is the article mainly about?
questions.explanation: 首段即報導 Cedar & Bean 將於今年秋天在日本大阪開設第一家海外咖啡館，全文圍繞其擴張。
questions.tip: 新聞體裁的主旨＝標題＋首段導言，30 秒內可鎖定。

questions.question: According to Ms. Vega, what has contributed to the company's success?
questions.explanation: 第二段 Vega 表示成長歸功於「direct partnerships with small farms」，以高於市價收購取得獨家豆源。
questions.tip: According to + 人名 → 找該人被引述（"..." she said）的那句話。

questions.question: What is indicated about Mr. Sato?
options: He previously operated a tea shop.
questions.explanation: 第三段：Kenji Sato 三年前加入公司，之前「running his own tea shop」，曾自營茶店。
questions.tip: 人物題把每個人名的修飾子句（who...）讀清楚，選項常把不同人的資訊互換設陷阱。

questions.question: The word "performs" in paragraph 4, line 1, is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: 「if the Osaka café performs well」指店面「營運表現良好」，故與 operates successfully 最接近。
questions.tip: 同義詞題一定要代回原句驗證，不要憑單字最常見的意思作答。

passages.label: 第一篇：研討會議程 (Schedule)
passages.text:
GREENTECH SUMMIT 2026 — DAY 1 PROGRAM (October 15)
Harborside Convention Center

9:00 A.M. — Opening Keynote: The Future of Clean Energy (Main Hall)
10:30 A.M. — Workshop A: Solar Solutions for Small Businesses (Room 201)
10:30 A.M. — Workshop B: Green Building Materials (Room 202)
1:00 P.M. — Panel Discussion: Financing Sustainable Startups (Main Hall)
3:00 P.M. — Workshop C: Reducing Office Waste (Room 201)
5:00 P.M. — Networking Reception (Rooftop Terrace)

Note: Workshops are limited to 40 participants each and require advance sign-up through the summit mobile app.

passages.label: 第二篇：電子郵件 (E-mail)
passages.text:
To: Priya Raman <p.raman@vextra.com>
From: Daniel Kim <d.kim@vextra.com>
Date: October 13
Subject: Summit on Wednesday

Hi Priya,

I've finished registering us both for the summit. I signed you up for the 10:30 session in Room 202 as you asked — my own first choice was already full, so I'll join the one on office waste in the afternoon instead.

One more thing: our CEO wants at least one of us at the finance panel to take notes, since we're looking for investors next year. Could you cover that? I have a client call at that time.

I'll drive, so let's meet in the parking garage at 8:15. The keynote starts at nine and I don't want to miss the opening.

Daniel

questions.question: What workshop did Ms. Raman ask to attend?
questions.explanation: 郵件說「I signed you up for the 10:30 session in Room 202」，對照議程 Room 202 的 10:30 場次是 Workshop B: Green Building Materials。
questions.tip: 雙篇必考「跨篇對照」：郵件給房號＋時間，議程給場次名稱，兩篇拼起來才有答案。

questions.question: What is suggested about Workshop A?
options: It was moved to the Main Hall.
questions.explanation: Daniel 說「my own first choice was already full」，而他改去下午的 office waste 場（Workshop C），可推知他原本想參加的上午另一場 Workshop A 已額滿（每場限 40 人）。
questions.tip: suggested / implied 題要推論：full ＝ 達到 40 人上限的改寫。

questions.question: What does Mr. Kim ask Ms. Raman to do at 1:00 P.M.?
options: Meet him in the parking garage
Take notes at the panel discussion
questions.explanation: 郵件請 Priya 代表出席 finance panel 做筆記；對照議程，該座談時間為下午一點。
questions.tip: 題目給時間、文章給活動名稱時，先回議程把時間換成活動，再回郵件找對應要求。

questions.question: Why does Mr. Kim want to arrive by 8:15 A.M.?
options: To be on time for the keynote
questions.explanation: 他說九點開場的 keynote「I don't want to miss the opening」，因此提早於 8:15 會合。
questions.tip: because/so 沒明寫時，看前後句的因果安排即可。

questions.question: In the e-mail, the word "cover" in paragraph 2, line 3, is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: 「Could you cover that?」意為「你能負責／處理那件事嗎」，與 handle 同義。
questions.tip: cover 多義：報導、支付、代班、覆蓋。代回原句選最通順者。

passages.label: 第一篇：網路廣告 (Advertisement)
passages.text:
OFFICEPRO SUPPLY — SPRING CLEARANCE

Ergonomic task chair (Model TC-40): $129 (regularly $189)
Adjustable standing desk (Model SD-12): $349 (regularly $449)
LED desk lamp (Model DL-3): $39 (regularly $55)
Monitor stand, bamboo (Model MS-7): $25 (regularly $40)

Orders over $500 qualify for free delivery within the metropolitan area. All clearance items carry a one-year warranty. Returns accepted within 30 days of delivery; clearance items may be exchanged but are not eligible for cash refunds.

Order online at www.officeprosupply.com.

passages.label: 第二篇：電子郵件 (E-mail)
passages.text:
To: Customer Service <service@officeprosupply.com>
From: Marcus Hale <m.hale@bluefirm.com>
Date: April 22
Subject: Order #88214

To Whom It May Concern:

On April 15, I ordered two ergonomic task chairs, one adjustable standing desk, and one LED desk lamp for our new branch office. The order arrived promptly on April 18, and we were pleased that delivery was free of charge.

However, when we unpacked the desk, we found that the height-adjustment motor does not work. Since your policy does not allow cash refunds on these items, I would like to exchange it for the same model. Could you also confirm whether the replacement will still be covered by the original warranty?

Please let me know how to proceed.

Marcus Hale, Office Manager, Blue Firm Consulting

questions.question: What is the total regular price of one TC-40 chair and one DL-3 lamp?
questions.explanation: regularly（原價）：椅子 $189 + 檯燈 $55 = $244。注意題目問原價而非特價（$129+$39=$168 是陷阱）。
questions.tip: 價格計算題先確認問的是 sale price 還是 regular price，再動手加。

questions.question: Why did Mr. Hale's order qualify for free delivery?
options: He ordered during a holiday promotion.
He picked up part of the order himself.
questions.explanation: 跨篇對照：訂單為椅子×2（$258）＋站立桌 $349＋檯燈 $39 = $646，超過廣告的 $500 免運門檻。
questions.tip: 雙篇經典題：郵件列品項、廣告列價格與門檻，必須自己算總額。

questions.question: What problem does Mr. Hale report?
options: An item arrived later than promised.
A chair was the wrong color.
questions.explanation: 郵件第二段：站立桌的「height-adjustment motor does not work」，升降馬達故障。
questions.tip: However 之後才是問題所在，前面多半是鋪陳與客套。

questions.question: What does Mr. Hale request?
options: A discount on a future order
An exchange for the same model
questions.explanation: 因清倉商品不可退現金，他要求「exchange it for the same model」換同型號新品，並詢問保固是否延續。
questions.tip: request 題找 would like to / could you 開頭的句子。

questions.question: What is indicated about the clearance items?
options: They can be returned for cash within 30 days.
They come with a one-year warranty.
They are only sold in stores.
They are limited to one per customer.
questions.explanation: 廣告載明「All clearance items carry a one-year warranty」；清倉品可換貨但不可退現金，故 (A) 錯。
questions.tip: indicated 題逐選項回文驗證，一半選項會是把條款反過來寫的陷阱。

passages.label: 第一篇：飯店政策 (Policy)
passages.text:
LAKESIDE GRAND HOTEL — BILLING & CANCELLATION POLICY

- Reservations canceled at least 48 hours before check-in incur no charge.
- Cancellations within 48 hours of check-in are charged one night's room rate.
- A $30 per night fee applies to rooms with lake-view upgrade.
- Corporate-rate guests receive complimentary breakfast and free parking.
- Disputed charges must be reported within 14 days of checkout by e-mailing billing@lakesidegrand.com.

passages.label: 第二篇：住宿帳單 (Invoice)
passages.text:
LAKESIDE GRAND HOTEL — INVOICE #20941
Guest: Sofia Marino, Corvex Industries (corporate rate)
Check-in: July 8 / Checkout: July 11 (3 nights)

Room charge (3 nights × $140): $420.00
Lake-view upgrade (3 nights × $30): $90.00
Breakfast (3 × $18): $54.00
Parking (3 × $15): $45.00
City tax: $21.00
TOTAL: $630.00

passages.label: 第三篇：電子郵件 (E-mail)
passages.text:
To: billing@lakesidegrand.com
From: Sofia Marino <s.marino@corvex.com>
Date: July 15
Subject: Invoice #20941 — incorrect charges

Dear Billing Department,

I stayed at your hotel from July 8 to July 11 under my company's corporate rate. While the room and upgrade charges are correct, my invoice includes two items that should not have been billed to me according to your own policy.

I did enjoy the lake view very much — the upgrade was well worth it. However, I would appreciate a corrected invoice and a refund of the difference to my credit card.

I am submitting this request today, four days after my checkout, so I trust it falls within the required reporting window.

Best regards,
Sofia Marino

questions.question: According to the policy, when can a guest cancel without being charged?
questions.explanation: 政策第一條：入住前 48 小時以上取消不收費。
questions.tip: 政策／規定類文章，題目多半照條列順序出，先掃條列再看題。

questions.question: Which charges does Ms. Marino most likely dispute?
questions.explanation: 三篇對照：政策載明 corporate-rate 房客享免費早餐與免費停車，但帳單卻收了 Breakfast $54 與 Parking $45，郵件也說房費與升等費正確，故爭議項目是早餐與停車費。
questions.tip: 三篇題王道：政策（該免費）＋帳單（卻收費）＋郵件（說哪些正確），三方交叉才鎖定答案。

questions.question: How much should Ms. Marino be refunded?
questions.tip: 退款金額題＝把上一題認定的錯誤項目加總，兩題通常連動。

questions.question: What is suggested about Ms. Marino's request?
options: It was submitted within the reporting deadline.
It was sent to the wrong department.
It arrived too late to be processed.
questions.explanation: 退房日 7/11，郵件寄於 7/15（退房後四天），符合政策「14 天內申訴」的期限。
questions.tip: 日期推算題把 checkout 日與寄信日圈起來相減，再對照政策天數。

questions.question: In the e-mail, what does Ms. Marino indicate about the lake-view upgrade?
options: It was worth the extra cost.
questions.explanation: 她寫道「the upgrade was well worth it」，認為升等值回票價，且未對此費用提出異議。
questions.tip: 別假設客訴信裡每個項目都在抱怨；先確認作者對各項目的態度。

passages.label: 電子郵件 (E-mail)
passages.text:
From: Rentals Department, CityBike Co.
To: Julia Moreno
Subject: Your annual membership

Dear Ms. Moreno,
